prTitle: fix(workflow): pause zero-tool-call retries on transient provider errors (closes #138)
prBody:
## Summary

Ports upstream `97d4ddb` (open-gsd/gsd-pi). Closes #138.

Zero-tool-call unit completions whose **last assistant message is a transient provider/rate-limit error** were being retried immediately, causing a CPU-spin loop. This adds a pre-emptive classification branch inside the existing zero-tool-call guard in `auto/phases.ts`: when the last assistant text classifies as a transient provider error (`rate-limit`/`network`/`stream`/`connection`/`server`), the loop now **pauses with backoff and schedules auto-resume** instead of redispatching.

## Changes

- `src/resources/extensions/workflow/auto/phases.ts`
  - New import of `classifyError` / `isTransient` from `../error-classifier.js`.
  - New `extractLastAssistantText` helper + `TRANSIENT_PROVIDER_MESSAGE_KINDS` constant (verbatim from upstream).
  - New provider-message-classification branch inside `if (lastUnit && lastUnit.toolCalls === 0)`, before the `USER_DRIVEN_DEEP_UNITS` check — pauses via `pauseAutoForProviderError` + `resumeAutoAfterProviderDelay`, emits a cancelled unit-end, and breaks with `rate-limit`/`api-timeout`.
- `src/resources/extensions/workflow/tests/auto-loop.test.ts`
  - Regression test (`#138`) pinning the root behavior: a zero-tool-call unit with a transient rate-limit assistant message pauses with a delayed auto-resume instead of immediately redispatching. Adapted to otto's mock harness.

## Strategy

`fix-strategy:direct-merge` — all dependencies exist in otto-cli with the same names; otto paths are `workflow/` vs upstream `gsd/`.

## Gates (all green)

- Regression: fail-before / pass-after ✅
- Build (tsc) ✅
- Targeted suite ✅
- Full suite (`npm test` → `npm run verify:pr`) ✅
- Independent reviewer: approved ✅
